sections.paragraph:
-

(1) to incline, e.g. a vessel, which is to be emptied, Jer 48:12. (Arab. صغى id. Eth. ከዐወ፡ to pour out, כ and צ being interchanged, see last remark under , p. 698, A ).

(2) to be inclined, bent, used of a man in bonds, Isa 51:14 also to bow oneself (ad concubitum), κατακλίνεσθαι, Jer 2:20.

(3) to bend back the head, i.e. to be proud, Isa 63:1.

Piel, i.q. Kal No. 1, ibid.
